name: Grilled Swordfish with Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Place fish in a single layer in a shallow baking dish.
2. Combine 1/2 cup catsup, lemon juice, oil, Worcestershire sauce, and garlic in a bowl.
3. Pour the sauce over the fish. Cover and refrigerate about 1 hour, turning the fish once.
4. Remove the fish and reserve the sauce. Use half of the reserved sauce for brushing the fish while grilling.
5. Combine the remaining half of the sauce with the remaining catsup, onion, water, sugar, hot pepper sauce, and bay leaf in a small bowl. Simmer about 20 minutes to blend flavors and thicken.
6. Place the fish on well-greased, hinged wire grills. (If barbecuing fish on a standard grill, brush the fish with oil before cooking.)
7. Cook over moderately hot coals for 8 minutes, basting with reserved sauce.
8. Turn the fish and cook an additional 7 to 8 minutes, or until it flakes easily when tested with a fork. Brush with more sauce as needed during cooking.
9. Serve by spooning the hot barbecue sauce over the fish.
